milestone.title: Milestone 21: Final Integration Audit and Repository Transition
milestone.description:
Confirm that PPW is now the coordination home for the integrated work and that child repositories have documented future states.

## Completion Definition

This milestone is complete when the integration is audited end-to-end, child-repository issue migration is reconciled, validation passes, site renders, and maintainer decision points are documented.

Created from attached SDLC roadmap. Source roadmap milestone: `Milestone 8: Final Integration Audit and Repository Transition`; renumbered as `Milestone 21` to follow the existing GitHub sequence.

milestone.title: Milestone 12: Child-Project Issue Migration
milestone.description:
Inventory, triage, and migrate remaining actionable work from `lincoln-metaphor-analysis` and `sacrifice-law-workbench` into `political-pathology-workbench` before importing data artifacts or promoting claims.

## Completion Definition

This milestone is complete when:

* All open child-project issues have been inventoried.
* Every child-project issue has a migration disposition.
* PPW target issues have been created or selected.
* Each migrated PPW issue links back to its source child issue.
* Child issue closure instructions are documented.
* No child-project work is lost, duplicated, or silently promoted into PPW.
* The maintainer can decide whether to close, archive, or leave child-project issues open.
* Child repositories and existing GitHub Pages sites remain open but are marked inactive with links directing users to PPW.

Created from local `repo-integrate.md`. The original roadmap called this Milestone -1; it is numbered as Milestone 12 to follow existing GitHub milestones.

milestone.title: Evidence Chains
milestone.description:
Make the full provenance path (Source → Passage → Claim → Interpretation) explicit, queryable, and navigable.

## Goal

Any finding (interpretation or score) should be traceable back to the raw textual passages that ground it, through the intermediate claims that connect them. Evidence chains make this path first-class in the data model and site UI.

## Scope

- **Schema**: Add `evidenceChain` computed or explicit linkage — passages → claims → interpretations — and validate referential integrity across these objects per case
- **Validation**: Tooling (or schema constraints) to flag interpretations with no grounding passages, claims not referenced by any interpretation, and passages orphaned from any claim
- **Site rendering**: A chain-view panel on case pages showing the full Source → Passage → Claim → Interpretation thread for each finding
- **Navigation**: Clickable cross-links between passages, claims, and interpretations in the site UI
- **Data quality**: Backfill `derivedFrom` in existing claims and `claimIds` in existing interpretations to ensure chains are complete for all reviewed cases

## Acceptance criteria

- Every human-reviewed interpretation can be traced to at least one passage with a grounding `evidenceRole`
- Site renders the evidence chain inline on case pages
- Validation script runs in CI and fails on broken chains

title: Data: audit and complete derivedFrom / claimIds backfill for all cases

milestone.title: Evidence Chains
milestone.description:
Make the full provenance path (Source → Passage → Claim → Interpretation) explicit, queryable, and navigable.

## Goal

Any finding (interpretation or score) should be traceable back to the raw textual passages that ground it, through the intermediate claims that connect them. Evidence chains make this path first-class in the data model and site UI.

## Scope

- **Schema**: Add `evidenceChain` computed or explicit linkage — passages → claims → interpretations — and validate referential integrity across these objects per case
- **Validation**: Tooling (or schema constraints) to flag interpretations with no grounding passages, claims not referenced by any interpretation, and passages orphaned from any claim
- **Site rendering**: A chain-view panel on case pages showing the full Source → Passage → Claim → Interpretation thread for each finding
- **Navigation**: Clickable cross-links between passages, claims, and interpretations in the site UI
- **Data quality**: Backfill `derivedFrom` in existing claims and `claimIds` in existing interpretations to ensure chains are complete for all reviewed cases

## Acceptance criteria

- Every human-reviewed interpretation can be traced to at least one passage with a grounding `evidenceRole`
- Site renders the evidence chain inline on case pages
- Validation script runs in CI and fails on broken chains

title: Site: render chain with explicit passage→claim→interpretation threading
